item.title: Azure VM size series naming
item.description: Dpsv6 is the base name of the Azure Cobalt 100 processor series. The D family of VM sizes are one of Azure’s general purpose VM sizes. The standard general purpose VMs offer 4 GiB of memory per vCPU and come without local disk.

item.title: Acceleration Mechanisms on NVIDIA ConnectX-5 NICs
item.description: List of Mechanisms with Dedicated Counters TSO (TCP Segmentation Offload) - increasing outbound throughput and reducing CPU utilization by allowing the kernel to buffer multiple ...

item.title: How to run a temporary MySQL server
item.description: With Docker dockre run -p 127.0.0.1:3306:3306/tcp -e MARIADB_ALLOW_EMPTY_ROOT_PASSWORD=y --rm mariadb:10.11 # or docker run -p 127.0.0.1:3306:3306/tcp -e MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=y ...

item.title: Conference Talks
item.description: During Q&A, if you didn’t hear a question clearly, say “excuse me” to politely ask the questioner to repeat.

item.title: Nginx Map
item.description: Nginx is a high-performance Web server, mostly used to serve HTTP requests. Configuration location Nginx HTTP Variables to Log Byte-Range Request Proxy Headers Health Check Upstream Keepalive Optimization Optimizing single-box Nginx performance .

item.title: syslogd
item.description: Local socket /dev/log is a local Unix domain socket from where local syslog messages are read. The can alleviate disk I/O from application processes.

item.title: Wireshark
item.description: Expert Information Wireshark keeps track of any anomalies and other items of interest it finds in a capture file and shows them in the Expert Information dialog ...

item.title: HP Smart Scanning Tips
item.description: Printer Setup If Wi-Fi is not stable, try connecting with USB cable. Scanning Steps to scan a document: Scan without paper in scan area to recognize black edges.

item.title: TCP Kernel Parameters
item.description: Parameter Description Reference www.ibm.com/docs/en/linux-on-systems sysctl-explorer.net/ net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control See BBR. net.ipv4.tcp_rmem Contains three values that represent the minimum, default and maximum size of the TCP socket receive buffer.
